transcript.text: Hi.
This meeting is being recorded.
Good morning.
How are you?
Can you hear me?
good.
How about yourself?
I can hear you.
Can you hear me?
Good.
Yeah, I think it's just a little delayed.
I'm good.
I have a really awful cold, so please ignore my voice and my Rudolph nose.
But other than that, all good.
Oh, I think Kathy.
Looks like Kathy is frozen, but good to meet you, Shannon.
I know we were chatting in LinkedIn a little bit, and then this call got scheduled, so I figured I'd crash the party.
Yes, I'm glad you did.
I am excited to learn more about what you all do, because I have never heard of GrowthX or even an agency that's similar to yours before Panther.
So I'd love to just start from the top, if that's okay with you all.
Yeah, that's...
sounds great.

And maybe we could just do like quick intros and I'd love to hear like more about your experience on the organic growth side and like any, you know, positive or negative feedback around how Aerox worked for you.
Because, you know, sometimes people are choosing like GrowthX or Aerox, like build versus buy, right?
But on my side, you know, I'm the founding AE at GrowthX and I work with our CEO, Marcel, pretty much every day.
And my background before this was starting in strategy consulting, moving to more like early stage startups.
And so like our model here is really interesting because it's almost like services as software because of how fast we can move, how quick we can start to generate results.
So excited to tell you a lot more about who we are and what we do after this.
Awesome.
Is this?
Oh, this is Deepgram, Marcel.
Marcel.
Yeah, you know, you know, Marcel.
I think I interviewed with him at one point years ago.
But then stayed connected.
so I knew that he laughed and started his own thing, but I didn't realize that this was that.
I'm kind of curious, who interviewed you?
Oh, he interviewed me.
This was in, must have been, yeah, like 2023-ish, before I joined Sourcegraph, I think.
But yeah, it was interesting chatting with him, and I've seen some LinkedIn updates, and so now I can put two and two together.
But I'm happy to do an intro as well, and keep it going that way, if that works.
Yeah.
Yeah, I would love to learn more about, you know, your path to Panther, and positive-negative stories about, like, what's worked with organic growth in the past.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay, so I came up in marketing through the demand-gen and ops side, so definitely more on the data-driven, you know, why.
What's going to lead to pipeline mindset?
I went to Palantir to build out DemandGen and was like, hey, we need the fuel for DemandGen.
We need product marketing.
need content.
We need all these other pieces that DemandGen uses to then create demand.
And that's how I ended up leading the marketing function for the most part at Palantir.
And then went back into the startup world, got into the AI dev tool space, which is just a whole different beast.
At Sourcegraph.
And it was very briefly at a company called Stig that's also in the AI monetization space.
Just they're an Israeli-based startup.
Wasn't a great fit.
So I was excited that Panther reached out with this role, more AI security-focused.
I love the security side as a marketer.
Security is a must-have always.
It's not a nice-to-have.
But there are plenty of options out there.
So it's really...

Really about how you as a marketer differentiate yourself and make aware that we are the thought leaders, that this is a very ever-changing space that people need to be educated on on a regular basis because you can't just say, I didn't know that this was a new hacker method or whatever.
It's not going to save you.
So that's why I love this space.
I'm happy to be at Panther on day two and just getting my feet wet, learning the team, learning the agencies, the tools, all of that.
Your earlier question about Aerofs and organic content, I didn't actually use Aerofs.
I almost brought them in.
I almost brought Profound in.
I just didn't buy it.
It felt like the data you were getting was now past its usefulness.
It was like the models are changing.
People are adapting.
And so it felt like a little bit too late, but I still liked the measurability.
I think
All CEOs, all VCs want to know, are you in machine?
What's your share?
All of that.
So I want to be able to answer that question.
I just didn't want to pay for it.

And then from a content perspective, what I truly believe in, it's true with DevOps, it's true with data scientists, and it's absolutely true with security engineers and security professionals, is you have to have content that speaks to them in their own language.
So my goal is to eventually bring on almost like a dev, like a dev rel person, but it's a security person who can help with the content.
So it's not either or in my mindset, it's like drive the direction and then help scale it out.
But content is absolutely critical to getting that right in many different forms.
So videos, blog posts, tutorials, docs, all of it, I believe in.
I wanted to ask a question.
Does Panther have a threat?
They sure do.
Okay, got it.
Yeah, that makes a ton of sense because I'm also from the developer security space.
And having someone locked in to do very deep technical things and showing, especially since Panther is such a developer, they love that product because of how deeply it integrates.

Being able to show the code, that really helps because I've been at GitHub, I've been at other companies where not just writing content that ranks well, but content that really speaks directly to the user as well as to the buyer.
They're very, very different.
It's extremely important.
Yeah, 100%.
And that's why it doesn't really work if it's the marketer doing the content.
And last, I've worked with more and more people that have done this.
You know, developer turned marketers, and then they have the cred to do it on one side, at least.
Excellent.
I guess I'll round out the set of intros.
So I'm Kathy.
Prior to GrowthX, I went up through marketing through more of the product and then the product marketing side.
I spent most of my time in the developer space, but I also started out my career mostly in security, application security, and SIM security.
So I know I have enough to get myself in trouble for security, but what's interesting is that the pain points don't change.
It was probably more than 10 years ago, and it was always about too much data.
Too much noise.
We don't know which ones is noise and which ones are false positives, false negatives.
And so to be back working with a company that is doing some really cool things with AI and with security, it's really solving the problem in a very unique way.
And so far, I've really enjoyed working with everyone on your team because they seem to really understand, like, oh, here is what we need to do.
Here's how we need to, here's the key KPIs to look for, like, are the things we're doing moving the needle.

And so I'm not sure if you had anyone introduce you to kind of our process, but the whole point of today was really to get you acquainted with us, see if there are, like, if you have any questions, any focus areas, so that we can kind of retweak things if necessary.
Yeah.
Excuse me.
So how much has Seema shared about the kind of repositioning effort that's underway?
I know we, a little bit.
So primarily, most of the calls that I've been having are primarily with Katie and Anna.
I know that in the past, the focus has been on more technical things.
And lately, because of the new AI features, we're trying to, Panther is trying to broaden the market to focus less on doing like more on like AI assisted.
So I think that's what we're aware of.
But if you have new messaging and new, like new content in that area, like we, we would love to get that download and incorporate that in the content we were writing.
Okay.
And.
Like, treat with care.

Like, maybe it's only shared with a certain email as opposed to, like, our team so that we can be more quick, right, with, like, support and not just have everything run through, like, one or two folks.
But, yeah, I guess I'm curious why you asked that, just to make sure we get everything taken care of.
You can speak to no one without an NDA, and so it would be, like, day one NDA.
But as I already shared, there is kind of a rebrand, refresh, repositioning effort underway.
I think it's true for many in our space.
And so I just wanted to make sure that there were some of those precautions put in place before we share the materials.
But that is top of mind for me.
And how I'm thinking about goals for this year is very much how we position ourselves as a thought leader within this.
New forming category in the AI SOC.
So that is what I would like for all of us to keep in mind as the goal and build content that will resonate.

And I try to take a two-prong approach and just simplify it to every big pillar that we want to focus on for each quarter and have the first be Jack, our founder, very thought leader, like he's well-respected, been there, done that.
But it's visionary.
And then the layer down is how do you actually do it?
And more technical, more practitioner level.
And I think we have people who can do it, but people who need to be doing other things.
And so that's where I think GrowthX could come in.
But it's a little bit different than I think your existing model, which is like take the data, then create content that fills that gap.
It's more of like a, like.
Follow the strategy versus the data, and I'd like to bring it together.
This is exactly why we have this meeting.
I can share you what the current strategy is right now, and then we will retweak it.
So let me, instead of sending you a document, sorry, go ahead, Tyler.

Before you do that, maybe I should just give Shannon like a quick five-minute, I know you know Marcel, but just a quick five-minute overview of like our process overall, and then we can make it Panther-specific for the rest of the call.
Does that sound more helpful?
Sounds good to me.
Up to you, though, Kathy, if you think it would be better to start with Panther.
Okay.
No, this is perfect.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I'll go real quick here and try to show you as much as I can, Shannon.
Since you know Marcel or like are aware of his background, I won't belabor this, but the idea is that like Marcel has always been an organic content guy, not really like a paid.
Advertising type of marketing leader.
And so he has a story right at every place he's been about a content engine he's built in some form or fashion.
And then at Deepgram, he automated where he could with AI.
So the deep research, generating briefs, thinking about assignments, structuring an outline, even drafting content.
And the results with how much faster he could move were so impressive that he started hosting these workshops.
He likes to build in public.
And it eventually just became that people didn't know how to start from zero and then refactor as models changed or like in a drag and drop sort of interface, right?
Like if one thing's broken, everything's broken.
How will cost scale if I'm using all these different APIs and processing tokens as I'm creating content?
And so what happened was he started GrowthX, right?
So that we could start to, we actually built our workflows on AirOps initially.
And then we built our own
Internal platform to just address some of the issues that we were facing with scale.
And so when you think about the partnership, I guess the first thing I'll say is that we actually are working with a ton of very technical B2B software clients.
I think security has been like a sweet spot for us as well.
And, you know, Kathy will get more into like what we've done for Panther specifically.
But for us, like we want to be an extension of your content team and we really want to optimize for scale.
Not to say like we want to put out content that's not at the right quality, but with AI search, we can get signals pretty quickly if we really just put some bets out there.
And we think about where's the search volume?
Where's the difficulty to rank?
What are the competitors doing?
And our approach is that we really just start with context.
So Kathy's done a great job in the strategy sprint, which is our phase one of building these artifacts, which I'll show you.
And the artifacts, it's around like your ICP or...
Company, your writing guidelines.
We'll create some custom ones as well.
This informs, like, how we personalize the content that we're creating.
But what's great is that there's experts in the loop for, like, every step of the process, which I'll show you a little bit of as well.
And so we have this internal platform that we use, and we also have, like, an AI visibility platform that we're launching very soon.
Kathy, do we have Panthers, like, AI search tracking in?
Check that.
Okay, cool.
Yep.
And we've added custom prompts to it as well.
Okay, nice.
Maybe you can give Shannon a quick look at that as well.

But the vision is that, like, everything would be connected in the internal platform that our team uses to execute, and then the AI visibility platform that your team has access to see how, you know, things are improving with AI search.
I think one differentiator is just that, I mean, Kathy's a great example of this, right?
Like, our experts in the loop, they're like, mark...
Marketing leaders, growth leaders, writers, right?
People who have experience with SEO.
Our entire delivery team is led by our chief content officer, who was the editor-in-chief at TechCrunch for about a decade.
And so it's really like combining the best of both worlds.
And to get tactical, we can be pretty flexible as a partner, but we're typically starting on editorial, a lot of the content types that you see here.
And then over time, we can set up these custom pipelines.
And so like we might have an interesting programmatic use case, like what we did for Lovable.
We've actually built out this templates library for Lovable and are using their product to generate these real apps that people can then go in and preview or remix.
But our specialty, right, is like the text, being able to like optimize for search.
Some other just quick examples to show you.
So SentinelOne, we work with them and we've done a
A lot of content that's just Cybersecurity 101 for them.
Some of these are definitions, you know, some of these are listicles.
But then if you look at clients like Abnormal Security, there's a ton of different editorial content that we've created that's more like you're talking about, right?
Like a little AI forward and like being thought leaders around how the space is changing.
And then we've also done things like their cybersecurity glossary.
So we can like have a wide range in terms of the technical depth, but sometimes like there's an opportunity to just drive visibility by getting the basics out of the way, right?
So especially when you're early stage, as you know, right, sometimes it just helps to show those signals to Google and get more content indexed over time.
I'll show you the platform next, but do you have any questions that we can help answer?
Um, how, so you are using your, I definitely want to say platform.
But the actual content creation, can you walk me through that process?
Like the editorial, like let's say it's a blog post that's going live.
And can you just like start to finish?
How did we decide on what topic was?
How is it generated?
How is it refined?
And then from there, what's the process to get it live?
Yeah, I'll give you the quick and dirty version of this.
And then I think Kathy can give you a very specific to Panther scenario.
So last thing here is just that like with Webflow, we've created these definitions pages.
We've created their integrations pages.
So just want to show you that we can be very flexible in terms of what we execute on.
Now, to answer your question, I'm going to use Engine as an example.
We've created all these business travel guides for them here.
And the first thing that we're doing is we're actually doing the keyword analysis to understand, one, where should we place our bets?
And two, what are the topic clusters where we should concentrate?
All of the different content assignments that we execute.
And so you can see this is all like rooted in search volume.
Everything is tracked in this OS where we can start to prioritize from a huge backlog, like which content should we focus on this week, next week, where is the data pointing us to?

And so once we've got this and we've got our artifacts, again, those artifacts, at the end of the day, we're trying to be audience first and we're trying to be the best answer to the many questions, you know, that they might have.
And so, you know, engines industry specific, and we just go super deep into everything related to business travel, related to what makes engine the right choice to use this as context, right?
So we have these artifacts and the, we even have like case studies for engine where we can like weave in some of their customer stories.
We've got...
Writing guidelines that give good and bad examples, talk about how to structure the content.
And so we actually need very little from your team to create these as a V1, but a lot of what we're doing is calibrating, right?
Like, does this sound right?
Where is their specific feedback?
These are like living documents.
And so when we create content using the lovable guides as an example, what we do is we have these pipelines that exist.
And again, we can customize these as time goes on, but we're leveraging all of these inputs, these artifacts, and we're leveraging the instructions from our managing editors.

And then we're going in and we're actually creating the content through these workflows, but giving our team the ability to, you know, make changes throughout the content or even like take a paragraph.
Add an artifact.
So I could bring in like the personas and I could just say like add three bullet points that are focused on tailored to starting a lifestyle blog.
I don't know, I'm just making something up.
And so it's really like this combination of each step is building on itself.
The research is actually an input for the drafting.
The internal linking is going to look across your site and find the most relevant pages to crosslink to.
We can make sure that like the source links all come from authoritative sources and there's some instructions that we bring in.
With the fact checking, we actually take the entire article and we break it into passages and then do deep research and suggest rewrites.
If there's any nuance that our editors can then accept all the way down here to the image generation.
Which we will, you know, do according to your brand guidelines and even like some infographics.
And so to give you like a, this is much longer than I intended, but to give you a quick look here, you know, every one of these steps, right, it's built in code.
So we can go in and start to see for the research, okay, what's actually happening in each step.
We're generating a research plan.
We've got all these different questions and what success looks like.
And then we synthesize the results.
And because these workflows are agentic, we can actually run this in a loop until we hit a certain quality threshold.
And then as you can see here, you know, all of these workflows, we have this catalog and there's like hundreds of workflows that we've created.
If you take that lovable example, you know, that's like a custom workflow here where we're ingesting all their docs.
And then we're actually going in and executing on specific steps related to that.
At custom use case, but for the deep research, I think this is a great one to just show quickly.
So for the deep research here, you can see that there was those artifacts as inputs, right?
You can see that there's some deep research happening for 32 minutes.
But then what you can't see here is that for each step, there's like this very complex prompt that's using the artifacts and that's helping us generate the right research plan.
You know, that's helping us synthesize the results or evaluate the quality in these different bands.
So like the short story here is instead of starting from zero, you basically get to start from a finished pipeline that's driven visibility and traffic for our clients.
And then we can fine tune that with the artifacts or with the custom steps in the process.
Okay.
Okay.
Do you have any questions?
Yeah.
Well, what more like, not a question statement.
It feels like this is Jasper, but like highly technical with more data backing it, which I love.
Jasper is one of those like, oh, that could be cool, but never really saw the real value there.
The other is a true question.
I'm a big believer.
So this is not true all the time.
But a lot of the time, I don't necessarily care that everybody sees it, just that the right people see it.
And so how are you gauging like what success looks like from a metric standpoint?
Like I want to make sure that the people at the right accounts are the ones that are engaging and that we're optimizing for that.
I'm glad you asked that question.
We just met with marketing ops last week.
And what we're trying to do is a couple of things.
week.
ops last We We
Because one, we want to bring the right people to your site and then to click on like solution pages, the thing, the CTAs that you're looking for.
And so what we're doing is building up a dashboard so that as we're creating more of this content, we're also getting the analytics behind the scene to see is like, are those leading to more clicks?
Are those leading to opportunities?
Are they the right folks?
And so we're really tying in not just like how much traffic we're bringing in.
We're doing that as well.
On the other, on the flip side of it, we do have this AEO, GEO dashboard, and I can share with you right now.
But what it does is we've seeded the project with a number of different problems on what would, let me backtrack a little.
The first thing is to understand who's your ICP, what questions they have, what problems they have.
And then from there, we generate a bunch of questions on like, what would they really research for?
And so we've...
Seated our tool with all of these prompts to see how you're performing at the moment.
And as we're shipping out new articles, how quickly those articles are being picked up by Google, Claude, ChatGPT, and how often they're referring traffic to your site.
And so I can share with you right now what your dashboard looks like.
Give me one second.
I want to make sure that.
Are you seeing this check that dashboard?
Yeah.
Yes.
Perfect.
So before we began the, like, this whole sprint process, and I wanted to give you an overview of the sprint later.
Week 2?
Yes.
I mean, it, you're comparing with...
Folks like Splunk, Datadog, Sumulogic, who have had years to build up their content.
And so this is like us from like the current day, like today, right?
And so what I actually said to Seema and the team is, hey, if you're looking for a good score, the things we should look for are like, one, what is your competitive presence?
Like, so if someone types in, hey, what's a great AI SOC vendor?
Like who, if I am a, if I'm this size company, what should I be looking for?
And so what we're looking for is like, are you in like the top three of the recommendations when you're asking certain questions?
Is the sentiment positive?
What's the citations share?
So you obviously want that to be more, more than, you know, what, 3% or something like that.
And so to give you an idea of some of the questions we're tracking.
And so
It's things like, hey, Splunk versus Panther for AWS-heavy environments, like comparing AI capabilities of different sim solutions.
So it's very specific to what a Panther prospect might look for in one of the AI tools.
And so what we're trying to see is, as we're creating more content, are we getting Panther discovered and in those queries?
Let me pause there and see if that answered some of your questions.
Yes, but the five responses, does that mean that it was like a query five times?
Let me see where the five responses.
Oh, okay.
So we, I just literally loaded these questions a few days ago.
So
What we may have been doing is every day we've gone out and prompted this and seen what are the answers.
So it's not, this is like five people.
all are asking and then logging the responses.
I forgot that's how that works.
Exactly.
But then you had also asked a question.
Actually, let me pause here and just see if you have any questions about this.
Do we have access to this?
You will.
Currently, the team is working on developing the product.
And I believe we are shipping it in February.
Is that right, Tyler?
I think that's the timeline.
It should be like any day now.
But it's definitely a step up from before, Shannon, where we were using Scrunch and you didn't have access.
We would basically just do reporting for you.
But so one thing that you pointed out, I think.
This is a good story, right?
Augment code, when we started working with them, they had like no visibility.
Now, you know, they're right behind GitHub, they're ahead of Cursor, and we're tracking a ton of different prompts.
So the augment code use case, we went for scale.
We created these guides where there's all these different guides and on the SOC topic, you know, even AI governance framework for SOC 2 compliance.
There's like code snippets in the content we're creating.
So we've created those and then a lot of comparisons or like, you know, listicles around technology in the space.

We've done some other stuff with them as well, but the results have been pretty nice where, you know, when someone looks up like enterprise coding tools that are good for context understanding, that's augment code secret spot or secret sauce, right?
And we've got all these different citations of articles that we've created.
sort lines we晚лять.

mean, trying.
And their traffic has grown significantly, you know, since the start of our engagement with the paid ad spend going to pretty much zero.
I mean, what you showed me, it looked like it was only like three months, that original chart.
Yeah, I mean, combination of a hot space, right?
Everybody's looking into like, how can I use AI for coding?
But yes, like six months was what led to the 3x in traffic for us.
And cursor is still winning like that.
So that's the other part of this is like, I've got to take it slow.
I just want to, I want the money.
I want the dollars in the door.
And right now, cursor is winning, even though Augment is catching up.
So like, is Augment, what is the effect on their pipeline, I wonder?
But anyway, and that's a shower thought, not a helpful thought.
And my apologies, Shannon, I didn't realize we were, this would go over.
I unfortunately have another meeting at the moment.
I know Tyler can answer additional questions.
But I would love to really do a deep dive and show you like, hey, this is how we set the strategy.
This is all the things we did.
And then get you acquainted with all the different dashboards.
I know this is still your first week.
Unfortunately, I'm also on a break soon where I'm actually traveling to visit some family.
So, yeah, if there are any questions, I can answer them now.
But I'll also be available through Slack.
And I'm also available later today if you want to continue this as well.
Yeah, I'll message you.
I don't have much time.
Like, we need to move very fast on this repositioning effort.
So I will definitely Slack you today.
And then hopefully we can meet sometime soon.
Excellent.
Thanks so much, Shannon.
Thank you.
Hi.
Nice to meet you.
Yeah, great to meet you, Shannon.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce Shannon King to GrowthX and align on content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=518.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Pivot:** Panther is repositioning as an AI SOC thought leader. The content strategy must support this by balancing visionary content (from the founder) with deep technical guides (from GrowthX).](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=711.0)
  - [**Targeted Success:** The goal is high-quality, technical content that resonates with security engineers and drives pipeline from the *right* accounts, not just high traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1523.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Role:** GrowthX will act as a "services as software" partner, using its AI platform to scale content production while incorporating Panther's new strategic direction.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=904.0)
  - [**Measurement Focus:** Success will be measured by AI search visibility (e.g., citations share, competitive presence) and its direct impact on the sales pipeline, not just organic traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1523.0)

## Topics

### Panther's New Content Strategy

  - [Shannon King (new Head of Marketing) outlined a new content strategy to support Panther's repositioning as an AI SOC thought leader.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=711.0)
  - [**Rationale:** The security space is a "must-have" market where differentiation through thought leadership and education is critical.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=230.0)
  - [**Two-Pronged Approach:**](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=734.0)
      - [**Visionary:** High-level thought leadership from founder Jack.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=734.0)
      - [**Technical:** Deep, practitioner-level content (tutorials, docs, etc.) to be scaled by GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=755.0)
  - [**Content Authenticity:** Content must speak the technical language of security engineers. Shannon plans to hire a "security dev rel" to ensure this authenticity.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=310.0)
  - [**Past Agency Experience:** Shannon avoided agencies like Aerofs because their data-driven models felt reactive, producing insights after market trends had already shifted.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)

### GrowthX's AI-Powered Content Engine

  - [Tyler Pavlas presented GrowthX's "services as software" model, which combines AI automation with expert oversight.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Process Overview:**](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
    1.  [**Strategy Sprint:** Defines core artifacts (ICP, writing guidelines) to personalize content.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=945.0)
    2.  [**Keyword Analysis:** Identifies high-potential topics based on search volume and difficulty.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1184.0)
    3.  [**AI Pipeline:** Generates content (research, outlines, drafts) using custom workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1279.0)
    4.  [**Expert Oversight:** Editors provide quality control and fact-checking at each step.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1364.0)
  - [**Platform Functionality:**](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1386.0)
      - [**Agentic Workflows:** Pipelines run in a loop until a quality threshold is met.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1408.0)
      - [**Artifact Integration:** Editors can pull in personas, case studies, etc., to enrich content.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1300.0)
      - [**Automated Tasks:** Includes internal linking, authoritative source verification, and brand-compliant image generation.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1343.0)
  - [**Example:** Augment Code achieved a 3x traffic increase in 6 months by scaling technical guides and comparisons, which significantly boosted their AI search visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1860.0)

### Measuring Success: AI Search & Pipeline Impact

  - [Shannon emphasized that success means engaging the *right* people at target accounts, not just maximizing traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1523.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Measurement Tools:**](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1590.0)
      - [**AI Visibility Platform:** Tracks Panther's performance in AI search results (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude) for specific, high-intent queries.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1590.0)
          - [**Metrics:** Competitive presence, sentiment, and citation share.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1694.0)
          - [**Queries:** Examples include "Splunk vs. Panther for AWS-heavy environments."](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1734.0)
          - [**Availability:** The platform is launching in February, giving Panther direct access.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1829.0)
      - [**Pipeline Dashboard:** A new dashboard is being built to connect content performance directly to business outcomes (clicks, opportunities, revenue).](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1537.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Shannon King:**](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1969.0)
      - [Sign an NDA to enable sharing of new brand/repositioning materials.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=677.0)
      - [Slack Kathy Lam to schedule a follow-up deep-dive on Panther's current content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1969.0)
  - [**Kathy Lam:**](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=1969.0)
      - [Share the current Panther content strategy with Shannon King.](https://fathom.video/share/Rw6UpTEbwMh8YUQLK8iXV69PY3YnBvQ5?tab=summary&timestamp=785.0)

action_items.description: Slack Kathy re: follow-up; schedule deep dive on strategy/dashboards
